title: Quality / Competitive recommendations (progressive-improvement + competitor-research)
description: Output schema for the progressive-improvement-agent and competitor-research-agent. Mirrors recommendations.schema.json but adds a confidence score and a tier band that gates auto-implement vs request-confirmation.

pages_analyzed.description: For progressive-improvement: count of pages crawled.

competitors_analyzed.description: For competitor-research: list of competitor domains analyzed.

id.description: rec-001, rec-002, etc. Stable for the run; referenced by responses + dashboard.

category.description: Issue / opportunity category. Progressive-improvement: incorrect-categorization, broken-page, outdated-content, duplicate-content, missing-content, layout-issue, accessibility, performance, content-error, other. Competitor-research: parity-feature, competitive-advantage, ux-improvement, content-gap, marketing-positioning, monetization, integrations, other.

confidence.description: How confident the agent is that this rec is correct + safe to ship. 0.95+ = high enough for auto-pilot once the agent has a track record.

tier.description: auto = high-confidence + low-risk → eligible for auto-implementation when site config opts in. review = needs explicit human approval. experimental = speculative, longer odds.

evidence.description: Where on the site (or which competitor URL) the recommendation came from. Lets the human verify quickly.

competitor.description: Competitor-research only: which competitor inspired this rec (if any).

expected_impact.description: Plain-text expected impact (e.g., 'reduces bounce rate on /pantry by ~15%').
